input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to create a BenchmarkDotNet project that benchmarks AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount across net9.0 and net10.0. I'll create a benchmark project that multi-targets both frameworks.

Let me create a benchmark project that:
1. References the TextAnalyzer source code
2. Uses BenchmarkDotNet
3. Multi-targets net9.0 and net10.0
4. Benchmarks the three methods

I'll create the benchmark project in a subdirectory.

toolCalls.name: create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-96d41bbcb91945a9ac05a5f68d2c139a/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj b/tmp/sv-96d41bbcb91945a9ac05a5f68d2c139a/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-96d41bbcb91945a9ac05a5f68d2c139a/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj
@@ -1,0 +1,13 @@
+<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
+    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.*" />
+    <Compile Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+</Project>
+

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-96d41bbcb91945a9ac05a5f68d2c139a/Benchmarks && python3 -c "
import zipfile, re, sys
path = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/benchmarkdotnet/0.15.8/lib/net8.0/BenchmarkDotNet.dll'
with open(path, 'rb') as f:
    data = f.read()
# Search for strings near 'Net90' or 'Net10'
for m in re.finditer(b'Net[0-9]+_?0', data):
    print(m.start(), m.group().decode('ascii', errors='replace'))
" 2>&1 | sort -t' ' -k2 | uniq -f1 | tail -20
